metadata.description: Official library documentation plus spreadsheet quality and accessibility guidance.

python_libraries.name: openpyxl Documentation
python_libraries.relevance: Python library for reading/writing .xlsx with formatting, charts, and validation.

python_libraries.name: XlsxWriter Documentation
python_libraries.relevance: Write-only Python library with rich formatting, charts, and in-cell rich text styling.

python_libraries.name: pandas.DataFrame.to_excel
python_libraries.relevance: Export DataFrames to Excel; common for reporting and reproducible exports.

python_libraries.name: xlwings Documentation
python_libraries.relevance: Python-Excel automation (useful for complex, interactive Excel workflows).

nodejs_libraries.name: ExcelJS
nodejs_libraries.relevance: Node.js library for generating and editing .xlsx files with styles and formulas.

nodejs_libraries.name: SheetJS Documentation
nodejs_libraries.relevance: Spreadsheet parsing/writing across formats; useful for ingestion and exports.

quality_and_accessibility.name: Accessibility best practices with Excel spreadsheets (Microsoft Support)
quality_and_accessibility.relevance: Practical guidance for making spreadsheets usable for people with disabilities (structure, labels, checkers).

quality_and_accessibility.name: Office Open XML (ECMA-376)
quality_and_accessibility.relevance: The underlying standard behind .xlsx; useful for edge cases and interoperability.

quality_and_accessibility.name: Microsoft Open Specifications (Office formats)
quality_and_accessibility.relevance: Reference entry point for Office Open XML and related Office format specifications.
